author: Josh Larkin, CementOps AI
notes: Top 20 most-cited MSHA standards for cement manufacturing (SIC 3241) under 30 CFR Part 56. Ordered roughly by citation frequency. Penalty ranges are typical — actual penalties depend on negligence, gravity, S&S finding, history, and operator size.

citations.title: Moving machine parts — Guards
citations.description: Moving machine parts shall be guarded to protect persons from contacting gears, sprockets, chains, drive, head, tail, and takeup pulleys, flywheels, couplings, shafts, fan blades, and similar moving parts that can cause injury.
citations.inspector_focus: Inspectors walk conveyor galleries first. They look for any rotating part within 7 feet of a walking/working surface. Head pulleys, tail pulleys, and return idlers are the most common hits.
defense_strategies: Guard was temporarily removed for active maintenance with LOTO properly applied and area barricaded
Area was inaccessible to personnel during normal operations — no walking/working surface within reach
Moving part was not within the 7-foot reach zone from any walking/working surface
Condition was identified during the operator's own inspection and corrective action was in progress at time of MSHA inspection
Guard was in place but inspector determined it was inadequate — contest the adequacy determination with manufacturer specifications

citations.title: Construction and maintenance of guards
citations.description: Guards shall be constructed and maintained to withstand the vibration, shock, and wear to which they will be subjected during normal operations.
citations.inspector_focus: Guards that are bent, cracked, have holes worn through, or are secured with wire/zip ties instead of proper fasteners. Vibration damage is extremely common on cement plant equipment.
defense_strategies: Guard met or exceeded manufacturer specifications at time of installation
Damage was recent (current shift) and had not yet been discovered through the normal inspection cycle
Guard was functionally adequate for its intended purpose despite cosmetic damage — no access to moving parts was possible
Replacement guard was on order and temporary barricading was in place

citations.title: Housekeeping
citations.description: At all mining operations, workplaces, passageways, storerooms, and service rooms shall be kept clean and orderly.
common_locations: everywhere — this is the #1 most frequently cited standard across all MSHA inspections
citations.inspector_focus: Material accumulation on walkways, spilled material not cleaned up, tools and hoses on walking surfaces, poor general cleanliness. Cement plants generate dust and spillage constantly — inspectors know this and still cite it.
defense_strategies: Active cleanup was in progress at time of inspection — show cleanup schedule and crew assignment
Accumulation was from current shift operations and within the normal cleanup schedule window
Area was barricaded or coned off pending scheduled cleanup
Show documented housekeeping program with assigned responsibilities and frequencies

citations.title: Safety defects — equipment, machinery, and tools shall be maintained in safe operating condition
citations.description: Equipment defects affecting safety shall be corrected before the equipment is used.
citations.inspector_focus: Broken handrails, damaged ladders, leaking hydraulic lines, worn belts, damaged tires on mobile equipment, inoperable lights, broken mirrors. Anything that should be on a PM checklist but was missed.
defense_strategies: Defect was identified in the pre-shift inspection and a work order was generated — equipment was not being operated
Defect did not affect the safe operation of the equipment (cosmetic only)
Equipment was locked out/tagged out pending repair at time of inspection
Show PM records demonstrating the defect was not present at last inspection

citations.title: Safety belts and lines
citations.description: Safety belts and lines shall be worn when persons work where there is a danger of falling.
citations.inspector_focus: Anyone working above 6 feet without fall protection — guardrails OR personal fall arrest. Preheater tower work is the most common hit in cement plants. Also watch for incomplete guardrail systems where one section is removed for access.
defense_strategies: Adequate guardrails were in place making personal fall arrest unnecessary
Work was performed from a properly rated man-lift or aerial platform with built-in fall restraint
Personnel were tied off but the anchor point was not visible from the inspector's vantage point
Person was transiting the area (not working) and guardrails were present along the travel path

citations.title: Safe access
citations.description: Safe means of access shall be provided and maintained to all working places.
citations.inspector_focus: Missing or damaged stair treads, ladder rungs, platforms with holes, access routes blocked by material or equipment, climbing on equipment instead of using provided access.
defense_strategies: Alternative safe access route was available and designated
Damage was recent and repair was scheduled/in progress
Area was barricaded to prevent use pending repair
Show that access was adequate for the task being performed

citations.title: Electrically powered equipment — Deenergization and lockout/tagout
citations.description: Electrically powered equipment shall be deenergized before mechanical work is done on such equipment. Power switches shall be locked out or other measures taken to prevent the equipment from being energized without the knowledge of the persons working on it.
citations.inspector_focus: LOTO is an automatic S&S in most cases. Inspectors check: are locks physically on the disconnect? Is there one lock per worker? Are tags filled out correctly? Has zero-energy verification been performed? Are all energy sources (electrical, hydraulic, pneumatic, gravity, thermal) addressed?
defense_strategies: LOTO was properly applied but lock/tag was not visible from the inspector's position
Work being performed was diagnostic/testing that required the equipment to be energized per manufacturer procedure
Equipment was deenergized through an alternative verified method (remote disconnect with verification)
Show LOTO program documentation, training records, and audit history

citations.title: Electrical work — qualified person
citations.description: Only qualified persons shall install, maintain, or modify electrical equipment.
citations.inspector_focus: Unqualified persons performing electrical work, improper wiring practices, open junction boxes, exposed wiring, improper grounding. Also cited when someone who is not a qualified electrician is found working inside an electrical panel.
defense_strategies: Person was qualified — show training records, certifications, and experience documentation
Work was being supervised by a qualified person who was present
The task being performed did not constitute electrical work (e.g., operating a disconnect switch)

citations.title: Roll-over protective structures (ROPS)
citations.description: Equipment included in the ROPS requirements shall be provided with ROPS.
citations.inspector_focus: Missing or modified ROPS, damaged ROPS that hasn't been recertified, ROPS with cracks or welds that aren't manufacturer-approved.
defense_strategies: ROPS met manufacturer specifications and was not modified
Equipment was only operated on level surfaces in controlled conditions (limited defense)
Show maintenance records and inspection history for ROPS integrity

citations.title: Seat belts
citations.description: Seat belts shall be provided and worn on mobile equipment that is equipped with ROPS.
citations.inspector_focus: Driver not wearing seatbelt, seatbelt damaged/inoperable, seatbelt retractor not working. Inspectors watch from a distance — if they see a driver exit without unbuckling, they cite it.
defense_strategies: Seatbelt was worn but was momentarily unbuckled for a specific task (very limited defense)
Show documented seatbelt policy, training records, and disciplinary history for violations
Equipment was stationary and not in operation at time of observation

citations.title: Horns and backup alarms on mobile equipment
citations.description: Mobile equipment shall be equipped with a horn or other audible warning device.
citations.inspector_focus: Non-functioning backup alarms (most common), horn not working, alarm not audible above ambient plant noise. Inspectors often test alarms by asking the operator to demonstrate.
defense_strategies: Alarm was functional — demonstrate that ambient noise conditions require a different audible level
Defect was identified on the pre-shift inspection and equipment was being taken out of service
Spotter system was in use as an equivalent safety measure (limited acceptance)

citations.title: Confined spaces — entry procedures
citations.description: Before any person enters a confined space, the atmosphere shall be tested for adequate oxygen and the absence of harmful gases.
citations.inspector_focus: Entry without atmospheric testing, entry without a permit, no attendant posted outside, no rescue plan, expired or uncalibrated gas monitors. Confined space violations in cement plants frequently involve silo and bin entry.
defense_strategies: Atmospheric testing was performed but documentation was not immediately available to the inspector — produce the records
Space did not meet the MSHA definition of a confined space (contest the classification)
Show comprehensive confined space program including permits, training records, rescue plan, and calibration logs for gas monitors

citations.title: Illumination
citations.description: Illumination sufficient to provide safe working conditions shall be provided in and on all surface structures, paths, walkways, stairways, switch panels, loading and dumping sites, and work areas.
citations.inspector_focus: Burned-out lights not replaced, dark stairways, inadequate lighting in work areas. Very common on night shift inspections. Inspectors carry light meters.
defense_strategies: Lights were operable — demonstrate that illumination meets the standard with a light meter reading
Bulb failure was recent (current shift) and replacement was scheduled
Portable/temporary lighting was available and in use in the work area

citations.title: Fire extinguishers — availability
citations.description: Fire extinguishers shall be available at locations where fire hazards exist.
citations.inspector_focus: Missing extinguishers, expired inspection tags, blocked access to extinguishers, wrong type for the hazard (e.g., water extinguisher near electrical), discharged extinguishers not replaced.
defense_strategies: Extinguisher was present but the inspector did not see it due to the location
Inspection was current but the tag was damaged/illegible — produce the inspection log
Extinguisher was temporarily removed for annual servicing with a replacement staged nearby

citations.title: Wall, bank, and slope stability
citations.description: Overhanging walls and banks shall be taken down or supported before other work is performed in the area.
citations.inspector_focus: Overhanging quarry walls, unsupported banks, personnel working at the base of unstable slopes, stockpile undermining.
defense_strategies: Ground control plan was in effect and the condition was within acceptable parameters
Area was barricaded and no personnel were exposed
Condition was identified and corrective scaling/benching was in progress

citations.title: Respiratory protection — respirable dust (silica)
citations.description: Respiratory protection shall be provided when exposure exceeds permissible limits.
common_locations: any area with airborne cement or silica dust
citations.inspector_focus: MSHA conducts personal dust sampling. If results exceed the PEL for respirable dust or silica, citations follow. Also cited: no respiratory protection program, fit testing not current, wrong cartridge type, damaged respirators.
defense_strategies: Engineering controls were in place and functioning (dust collection, wet suppression) — contest the sample methodology
Personal sampling data from the operator's own monitoring program shows compliance
Respiratory protection was available and in use — contest the exposure duration calculation
Show comprehensive respiratory protection program including medical evaluations, fit testing, and training

citations.title: Protective equipment — hard hats, eye protection, etc.
citations.description: All persons shall wear suitable hard hats when in or around a mine where falling objects may create a hazard.
citations.inspector_focus: Person not wearing required PPE in designated areas — hard hat, safety glasses, high-vis, hearing protection. Easy citation, often written alongside more serious violations.
defense_strategies: PPE was momentarily removed for a specific reason (very limited defense)
Person was in a designated PPE-exempt area
Show PPE policy, signage, training records, and disciplinary enforcement history

citations.title: Electrical conductors — insulation and protection
citations.description: Electrical conductors shall be properly insulated and protected from mechanical damage.
citations.inspector_focus: Damaged insulation on cables, exposed copper, cables run through doorways or across walkways without protection, improper splices, missing cable covers on junction boxes.
defense_strategies: Damage was recent and not yet identified through the normal inspection process
Cable was de-energized and tagged for repair
Show electrical inspection program and maintenance records

citations.title: Equipment defects affecting safety — correction required
citations.description: Equipment defects affecting safety shall be corrected in a timely manner to prevent hazards to persons.
common_locations: any equipment — this is a catch-all standard
citations.inspector_focus: This is MSHA's most versatile citation. Any defect that could affect safety — cracked welds, leaking hydraulics, worn brake pads, broken mirrors, malfunctioning safety interlocks. If it's broken and could hurt someone, this standard applies.
defense_strategies: Defect was identified and repair was in progress — show work order with timestamp
Equipment was locked out and not in service pending repair
Defect did not affect the safe operation under the conditions of use
Show preventive maintenance program and inspection records

citations.title: Examination of working places
citations.description: A competent person designated by the operator shall examine each working place at least once each shift for conditions which may adversely affect safety or health.
common_locations: plant-wide — this applies to every working area
citations.inspector_focus: No documented workplace examination, examinations not conducted each shift, obvious hazards that should have been caught by the examination (proves it wasn't done or wasn't adequate). Often cited alongside other violations — 'if you'd done your exam, you would have found this.'
defense_strategies: Examination was conducted — produce documentation with date, time, examiner name, and findings
Condition developed after the examination was completed (show timeline)
Show examination program including training, checklists, and corrective action tracking
